// 01 — About

Who I Am

Mechanical engineer with deep hands-on proficiency in PTC Creo Parametric and solid exposure to SolidWorks and Fusion 360. I combine strong fundamentals in mechanisms, kinematics, and manufacturing with a systematic design approach.

Currently pursuing M.Tech Smart Manufacturing at IISc Bengaluru (CGPA 8.2), where I've designed and physically fabricated compliant mechanisms using FDM 3D printing at the M2D2 Lab. Achieved AIR 150 in GATE 2025 (Engineering Sciences).

I care about building things that actually work — from precisely constrained industrial assemblies to printed grippers tested on real objects.

CAD / Modelling Creo Parametric · SolidWorks · Fusion 360 · GD&T · BOM
Simulation ANSYS Workbench (FEA) · Creo Mechanism
Manufacturing FDM 3D Printing · Sheet Metal · Machining · DfAM
Design Methods TRIZ · FMEA · Pugh Matrix · Morphological Analysis
Materials Steel · Composites · PLA · TPU · Cast Iron
Programming Python · NumPy · Pandas · Scikit-learn · MATLAB

// 02 — CAD Projects

Design Work

Five multi-component assemblies built in Creo Parametric — spanning industrial mechanisms, kinematic linkages, gear drive systems, and architectural structures.

01
CAD Assembly · Furniture

Contemporary Slatted Coffee Table

PTC Creo Parametric · 20+ Components

A furniture assembly designed to balance structural stability with visual lightness. The transparent glass top and cantilevered pillar design solve the bulkiness problem of traditional wooden furniture.

  • Parametric Linear Patterning: Shelf slats controlled by a single quantity parameter — fully scalable
  • Notched Joinery: Pillar recessed shoulders lock the glass top in place — no fasteners
  • Tangent & Coincident mates simulate real friction-fit assembly
  • Material rendering: Glossy wood grain vs. transparent glass for client-ready output
Creo Parametric Assembly Patterning Rendering
02
Industrial Mechanism · Valve

OS&Y Globe Valve Design & Assembly

PTC Creo Parametric · 15+ Components · Exploded View · 3 Cross-Sections

A complete Outside Screw & Yoke globe valve — the engineering solution to gate valve wire-drawing erosion. Provides precise throttling for steam, oil, and gas applications.

  • S-shaped flow path: Consistent wall thickness for high-pressure containment
  • OS&Y threading: Stem isolated from process fluid — prevents corrosion, extends life
  • Screw & Cylindrical mates synchronize handwheel rotation with linear stem travel
  • Cross-section validation confirms bubble-tight disc-to-seat sealing across full stroke
  • Exploded view documents assembly/disassembly sequence for field technicians
15+ Parts Sectioning Exploded View Motion Mates
03
Kinematics · Linkage Mechanism

Retractable Scissor Staircase Mechanism

PTC Creo Parametric · 40+ PIN30 Joints · Dual Rod Lengths

A multi-stage pantograph (scissor) linkage that collapses a full staircase into a ceiling cavity — reclaiming floor space in urban and tiny-home environments.

  • All joints synchronized: Every PIN30 constrained so treads stay horizontal throughout stroke
  • 200 mm & 280 mm connecting rods: Asymmetric lengths match real scissor-lift geometry
  • ~5:1 retraction ratio: Full staircase folds into compact overhead compartment
  • Pin & Slot Motor simulation validates self-weight support without binding
Kinematic Sim PIN Joints Pantograph Motion Validation
04
Drive System · Gear Train

Dual-Shaft Industrial Shredder

PTC Creo Parametric · 50+ Parts · Sheet Metal · Gear Drive

Counter-rotating dual-rotor shredder with synchronized spur gear drive — engineered to pull material into the crush zone and eliminate the jamming failure of single-shaft designs.

  • Hex-profile shafts: Maximise torque transmission, prevent blade slip under extreme loads
  • 1:1 spur gear synchronization: Perfect timing of interlocking dual cutters
  • Hooked blade profiles: Shear-and-tear action rather than simple crushing
  • BOM-ready structure: Grouped into SHAFTS / GEARS / KEYS / FASTENER for manufacturing docs
50+ Parts Gear Train Sheet Metal BOM Groups
05
Structural · Architectural

Modular Arch-Roof Shed System

PTC Creo Parametric · Pattern-Driven · Variable Section Sweep · IISc 2025

A parametrically scalable industrial shed where the entire structure length updates by changing one "Quantity" parameter — solving the rigidity of fixed-size shed designs.

  • Directional patterning: All pillars and roof ribs driven by a single parameter
  • Interlocking geometry: Arch profiles overlap and seal without custom fasteners per joint
  • Variable Section Sweep: Arched roof transitions and integrated gutter wings
  • Internal ribbing: High rigidity with minimised material — vertical load paths to foundations
Parametric Design Surface Modelling Structural

// 03 — Physical Prototypes

Built & Tested

Two compliant mechanisms physically fabricated using FDM 3D printing at IISc's M2D2 Lab — designed, printed, assembled, and tested on real objects. No hinges. No fasteners. Pure geometry.

06
DfAM · Robotics · Compliant Mechanism

Multi-Material Universal Compliant Gripper

Adapted the Non-Inverting Lever Compliant Mechanism (IISc-M2D2-CM library) into a functional two-jaw universal gripper. The co-directional motion topology — where input and output move in the same direction — provides symmetric jaw closure with balanced grip force. Zero hinges. Zero backlash. Six parts snap together by hand via dovetail joints.

Material
PLA + TPU
Parts
6 (3 PLA + 3 TPU)
Fasteners
None
Joints
Dovetail snap-fit
Max load
150 g (padlock)
Lab
M2D2, IISc
ObjectWeightGeometryResult
2-Rupee Coin~5 gThin flat disc✓ Held stably
Padlock~150 gCylindrical metal✓ Suspended in air
Tape Roll~40 gToroidal✓ Centred, no deform
Electrical Charger~100 gRectangular✓ No slip when tilted
07
DfAM · Consumer Product · Compliant Mechanism

Adjustable Compliant Nut Cracker

A Compliant Compression Mechanism adapted into a kitchen nut cracker with three jaw-slot levels (small / medium / large) — accommodating almonds, hazelnuts, and walnuts without adjustments or additional parts. The arch stores elastic energy during cracking and acts as the passive return spring. Two-part PLA print; entirely hinge-free and fastener-free.

Material
PLA only
Parts
2
Infill
25% Gyroid
Wall
4 mm
Orientation
Vertical (arch along layers)
Nut sizes
10 / 18 / 28 mm
NutSizeSlotResult

Hazelnut~18 mmMedium✓ Clean split
Walnut~28 mmLarge✓ Shell split, kernel whole
